ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa ŋaa wɩalɩŋ kala Wɩa sɩ sɩ ʋ ŋaa.
translated: The chief of Nowa has a big house like that of the chief of Wia.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋla pɩ Wɩa, “Dɩ ŋ nɛ jaŋ pɛ Ɩsɩmɛl lɛ.”
translated: And Abraham named his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Rakɛl sɩ lʋl Josɛf ʋ harɩŋ lɛ, Jekɔb bʋla pɩ Labaŋ, “Tamɛ dɩ mɩ mɩɩra mʋ mɩ jaŋ.
translated: Rachel saw that she was not bearing Jacob any children, so she became jealous of Leah, "Give me children, or I'll die."

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Ama ŋ nɛ bʋla arɩ ŋ jaŋ pɛ mɩ lɛ a leŋ dɩ mɩ doho nɩaa pʋŋ a nagɛ hagɩlɩ-bumbugulo sɩ hɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ niiŋ nʋʋ sɩ bɩ jaŋ wuo dɩɩsɛ.”
translated: But I will tell you that when I was going to farm, I saw a big tortoise and a big monitor lizard fighting on the path.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb pɔrɛ pie-bisi-la aŋ leŋ pie-kaanaa chaasɛ pʋsʋ-la sɩ bire arɩ kɩalɩŋ sɩ tagɩsa a ŋaa Labaŋ kɩaa. Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ ŋaa a kɛŋ ʋ tɩtɩa pʋsʋŋ. Ʋ bɩ ba joŋo guoli Labaŋ pʋsʋŋ lɛ.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa ŋaa wɩalɩŋ kala Wɩa sɩ sɩ ʋ ŋaa.
translated: The chief of Nowa has a big house like that of the chief of Wia.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋla pɩ Wɩa, “Dɩ ŋ nɛ jaŋ pɛ Ɩsɩmɛl lɛ.”
translated: And Abraham named his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Rakɛl sɩ lʋl Josɛf ʋ harɩŋ lɛ, Jekɔb bʋla pɩ Labaŋ, “Tamɛ dɩ mɩ mɩɩra mʋ mɩ jaŋ.
translated: Rachel saw that she was not bearing Jacob any children, so she became jealous of Leah, "Give me children, or I'll die."

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Ama ŋ nɛ bʋla arɩ ŋ jaŋ pɛ mɩ lɛ a leŋ dɩ mɩ doho nɩaa pʋŋ a nagɛ hagɩlɩ-bumbugulo sɩ hɛ mʋgʋ-balɩŋ niiŋ nʋʋ sɩ bɩ jaŋ wuo dɩɩsɛ.”
translated: But I will tell you that when I was going to farm, I saw a big tortoise and a big monitor lizard fighting on the path.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”

translated: The rabbit said to the monkey, "Let us go to the forest and look for food, and let us go to the river and catch fish, and let us go to the mountain and kill a duiker, and let us go to the bush and kill a bush pig, and let us go to the tree and pluck fruit."

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Nowa ŋaa wɩalɩŋ kala Wɩa sɩ sɩ ʋ ŋaa.
translated: The chief of Nowa has a big house like that of the chief of Wia.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩraham bʋla pɩ Wɩa, “Dɩ ŋ nɛ jaŋ pɛ Ɩsɩmɛl lɛ.”
translated: And Abraham named his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Rakɛl sɩ lʋl Josɛf ʋ harɩŋ lɛ, Jekɔb bʋla pɩ Labaŋ, “Tamɛ dɩ mɩ mɩɩra mʋ mɩ jaŋ.
translated: Rachel saw that she was not bearing Jacob any children, so she became jealous of Leah, "Give me children, or I'll die."

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.

translated: But I will tell you that when I was going to farm, I saw a big tortoise and a big monitor lizard fighting on the path.
